title: Adolescent/Youth Counseling * Native American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Adolescent/Youth Counseling * Native American Community 2 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Immigration Issues Native American Community Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE COOS, LOWER UMPQUA, AND SIUSLAW INDIANS THREE RINVERS BEHAVIORAL HEALTH COOS BAY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individual, couples, and family counseling, mental health evaluations, and medication management for both adult and pediatric patients. Additional support includes care coordination to help patients access services within the clinic and in the wider community. Also provides case management and peer support in partnership with tribal law enforcement to help individuals with behavioral health needs focus on recovery and avoid involvement with the criminal justice system. 250 Hull Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Tribal members of the Confederated Tribes of the Coos, Lower Umpqua and Siuslaw Indians and their widows/widowers Hours: Monday-Friday 7am-6pm Email (541) 435-7200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN REHABILITATION ASSOCIATION OF THE NORTHWEST NARA NW CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES GRESHAM CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health assessment and evaluation, counseling (individual, couples, family, and group), case management, cultural groups and activities, Native parenting classes, foster parent groups, access to physical health services, Healthy Native Kids (OHP/CHIPRA) insurance assistance, and wraparound care for Native youth with multiple needs. Referrals to other resources provided. Also provides diapers. Staff receive training on cultural competence to provide cultural programming for clients. Staff includes Master's-level counselors and social workers, certified alcohol and drug counselors (CADC), psychologists and child psychiatrists, and nurse practitioners. Student interns studying to become CADCs or counselors may also work under supervision of licensed staff. The program is certified by the State of Oregon to provide mental health and addiction treatment services for all ages. 620 NE 2nd Street Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Wraparound care: Youth who identify as Native American ages 9-21 All other services: Families with minor children who identify as Native American Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 953-6598 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE COOS, LOWER UMPQUA, AND SIUSLAW INDIANS BEHAVIORAL HEALTH NATIVE AMERICAN REHABILITATION ASSOCIATION OF THE NORTHWEST CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES
